Authors: Wenyuan Ying; Tianyang Dong; Libin Zhang; Jing Fan

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113482

Relevance: 0.766 (must_read)

Recently, single image super-resolution (SR) tasks have significantly benefited from the advances of deep neural networks. However, these methods often require burdensome memory and computation costs, which hinder their applications in real-world tasks (e.g., the practicality in edge devices). To address the challenge, we propose a lightweight frequency-driven lattice network (FDLN) adapting to low-computation costs for SR. Firstly, we construct a frequency-driven lattice block (FDLB) with enhanced network ability for processing high-frequency features. To enrich the network representation by promoting sensitivity to the frequency features, in the FDLB, we propose two novel frequency-driven reorganization methods: Channel-Wise Reorganization (CWR) and Global-Wise Reorganization (GWR). These methods bridge the frequency and spatial domains to adaptively guide network representation. Specifically, the module of CWR is designed to adaptively reorganize the network representation in channels based on the frequency features. Unlike CWR, the GWR uses the separated frequency features to provide adaptive global weights for information reorganization. Our proposed CWR and GWR are plug-in and require extremely low computational costs. The experimental results show that the PSNR/SSIM performance of our FDLN outperforms recent lightweight SR models with similar limitations of low computation costs and memory consumption. In the meantime, using our reorganization methods can also significantly improve the typical low-computation SR models with an imperceptible increase in computational costs.

item.title: SSSMN: Spatial-Spectral Sparse Mamba Network for Efficient Hyperspectral Fusion Super-resolution
item.dc:creator: Chenguo Feng
Haibo Liu
Renwei Dian
Yang Liu
Shutao Li
item.description: Hyperspectral fusion super-resolution (HFS) aims to produce high-resolution hyperspectral images (HR-HSIs) by integrating low-resolution hyperspectral images (LR-HSIs) with high-resolution multispectral images (HR-MSIs). Recently, selective state space-based Mamba has outperformed CNNs and Transformers in image fusion tasks due to its excellent global modeling ability and computational efficiency. However, Mamba struggles to comprehensively capture vital spatial-spectral features and is prone to forgetting early token information during recursive sequence scanning. To efficiently tackle the above challenges, this paper considers the spatial-spectral sparsity of HR-HSIs and proposes a spatial-spectral sparse Mamba network (SSSMN) for HFS tasks. Specifically, we first design a spatial-spectral sparse Mamba block (SSSMB) composed of a spatial sparse Mamba block (SpaSMB) and a spectral sparse Mamba block (SpeSMB). The SpaSMB employs spatial attention to induce top-k sparsity and performs bidirectional scanning on the sparse spatial features. Likewise, the SpeSMB adopts spectral attention to achieve top-k sparsity and scans the sparse spectral features bidirectionally. Then, a spectral attention-enhanced feature fusion block (FFB) is presented to effectively aggregate spatial-spectral features from different inputs. Finally, a series of SSSMBs and FFBs are stacked into a U-Net to mine the spatial-spectral properties of HSIs at various scales. Experiments on public HSI datasets indicate the excellent performance of our SSSMN in terms of fusion quality and computational complexity. The source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/hnu-VML/fcg . Published: 2026-03-14T23:32:35+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.765 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chenguo Feng; Haibo Liu; Renwei Dian; Yang Liu; Shutao Li

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113462

Relevance: 0.765 (must_read)

Hyperspectral fusion super-resolution (HFS) aims to produce high-resolution hyperspectral images (HR-HSIs) by integrating low-resolution hyperspectral images (LR-HSIs) with high-resolution multispectral images (HR-MSIs). Recently, selective state space-based Mamba has outperformed CNNs and Transformers in image fusion tasks due to its excellent global modeling ability and computational efficiency. However, Mamba struggles to comprehensively capture vital spatial-spectral features and is prone to forgetting early token information during recursive sequence scanning. To efficiently tackle the above challenges, this paper considers the spatial-spectral sparsity of HR-HSIs and proposes a spatial-spectral sparse Mamba network (SSSMN) for HFS tasks. Specifically, we first design a spatial-spectral sparse Mamba block (SSSMB) composed of a spatial sparse Mamba block (SpaSMB) and a spectral sparse Mamba block (SpeSMB). The SpaSMB employs spatial attention to induce top-k sparsity and performs bidirectional scanning on the sparse spatial features. Likewise, the SpeSMB adopts spectral attention to achieve top-k sparsity and scans the sparse spectral features bidirectionally. Then, a spectral attention-enhanced feature fusion block (FFB) is presented to effectively aggregate spatial-spectral features from different inputs. Finally, a series of SSSMBs and FFBs are stacked into a U-Net to mine the spatial-spectral properties of HSIs at various scales. Experiments on public HSI datasets indicate the excellent performance of our SSSMN in terms of fusion quality and computational complexity. The source code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/hnu-VML/fcg .

item.title: Task-aware All-in-one Guided Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Tingting Wang
Jun Wang
Qiuhai Yan
Junkang Zhang
Faming Fang
Guixu Zhang
item.description: Guided image super-resolution (GISR) aims to enhance the resolution of a low-resolution (LR) target image by leveraging complementary information from a high-resolution (HR) guidance image. However, due to the substantial modality diversity among GISR subtasks, most existing methods are tailored to individual subtasks, which significantly limits their generalizability and practical scalability. To address this limitation, we propose MAG-Net , the first all-in-one GISR framework capable of handling multiple GISR subtasks within a single unified model. MAG-Net is built upon a shared encoder-decoder backbone and integrates two key modules to handle heterogeneous input modalities and diverse task objectives. The Multi-modal Prompt Generation Module (MPGM) dynamically generates task-aware prompts by jointly encoding the input image pair and pre-defined textual task descriptions. These prompts serve as soft instructions, effectively capturing both visual features and textural cues, thus enabling the model to adaptively distinguish and respond to different GISR subtasks. The Multi-Guidance Routing Module (MGRM) is then designed to mitigate task interference and enhance feature specialization. This module leverages a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) strategy to adaptively route intermediate features through task-relevant expert branches, guided by the task-aware prompt and the characteristics of the guidance image. Extensive experiments across various GISR subtasks demonstrate that MAG-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in both all-in-one and one-by-one training settings. Code and pre-trained models will be released upon paper acceptance. Published: 2026-03-15T22:55:49+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.765 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Tingting Wang; Jun Wang; Qiuhai Yan; Junkang Zhang; Faming Fang; Guixu Zhang

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113483

Relevance: 0.765 (must_read)

Guided image super-resolution (GISR) aims to enhance the resolution of a low-resolution (LR) target image by leveraging complementary information from a high-resolution (HR) guidance image. However, due to the substantial modality diversity among GISR subtasks, most existing methods are tailored to individual subtasks, which significantly limits their generalizability and practical scalability. To address this limitation, we propose MAG-Net , the first all-in-one GISR framework capable of handling multiple GISR subtasks within a single unified model. MAG-Net is built upon a shared encoder-decoder backbone and integrates two key modules to handle heterogeneous input modalities and diverse task objectives. The Multi-modal Prompt Generation Module (MPGM) dynamically generates task-aware prompts by jointly encoding the input image pair and pre-defined textual task descriptions. These prompts serve as soft instructions, effectively capturing both visual features and textural cues, thus enabling the model to adaptively distinguish and respond to different GISR subtasks. The Multi-Guidance Routing Module (MGRM) is then designed to mitigate task interference and enhance feature specialization. This module leverages a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) strategy to adaptively route intermediate features through task-relevant expert branches, guided by the task-aware prompt and the characteristics of the guidance image. Extensive experiments across various GISR subtasks demonstrate that MAG-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in both all-in-one and one-by-one training settings. Code and pre-trained models will be released upon paper acceptance.

item.title: Noise-aware few-shot learning through bi-directional multi-view prompt alignment
item.dc:creator: Lu Niu
Cheng Xue
item.description: Vision-language models offer strong few-shot capability through prompt tuning but remain vulnerable to noisy labels, which can corrupt prompts and degrade cross-modal alignment. Existing approaches struggle because they often lack the ability to model fine-grained semantic cues and to adaptively separate clean from noisy signals. To address these challenges, we propose NA-MVP, a framework for Noise-Aware few-shot learning through bi-directional Multi-View Prompt alignment. NA-MVP is built upon a key conceptual shift: robust prompt learning requires moving from global matching to region-aware alignment that explicitly distinguishes clean cues from noisy ones. To realize this, NA-MVP employs (1) multi-view prompts combined with unbalanced optimal transport to achieve fine-grained patch-to-prompt correspondence while suppressing unreliable regions; (2) a bi-directional prompt design that captures complementary clean-oriented and noise-aware cues, enabling the model to focus on stable semantics; and (3) an alignment-guided selective refinement strategy that uses optimal transport to correct only mislabeled samples while retaining reliable data. Experiments on synthetic and real-world noisy benchmarks demonstrate that NA-MVP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, confirming its effectiveness in enabling robust few-shot learning under noisy supervision. Published: 2026-03-12T07:17:28+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.763 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Lu Niu; Cheng Xue

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.763 (consider)

Vision-language models offer strong few-shot capability through prompt tuning but remain vulnerable to noisy labels, which can corrupt prompts and degrade cross-modal alignment. Existing approaches struggle because they often lack the ability to model fine-grained semantic cues and to adaptively separate clean from noisy signals. To address these challenges, we propose NA-MVP, a framework for Noise-Aware few-shot learning through bi-directional Multi-View Prompt alignment. NA-MVP is built upon a key conceptual shift: robust prompt learning requires moving from global matching to region-aware alignment that explicitly distinguishes clean cues from noisy ones. To realize this, NA-MVP employs (1) multi-view prompts combined with unbalanced optimal transport to achieve fine-grained patch-to-prompt correspondence while suppressing unreliable regions; (2) a bi-directional prompt design that captures complementary clean-oriented and noise-aware cues, enabling the model to focus on stable semantics; and (3) an alignment-guided selective refinement strategy that uses optimal transport to correct only mislabeled samples while retaining reliable data. Experiments on synthetic and real-world noisy benchmarks demonstrate that NA-MVP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, confirming its effectiveness in enabling robust few-shot learning under noisy supervision.

item.title: HiAP: A Multi-Granular Stochastic Auto-Pruning Framework for Vision Transformers
item.dc:creator: Andy Li
Aiden Durrant
Milan Markovic
Georgios Leontidis
item.description: Vision Transformers require significant computational resources and memory bandwidth, severely limiting their deployment on edge devices. While recent structured pruning methods successfully reduce theoretical FLOPs, they typically operate at a single structural granularity and rely on complex, multi-stage pipelines with post-hoc thresholding to satisfy sparsity budgets. In this paper, we propose Hierarchical Auto-Pruning (HiAP), a continuous relaxation framework that discovers optimal sub-networks in a single end-to-end training phase without requiring manual importance heuristics or predefined per-layer sparsity targets. HiAP introduces stochastic Gumbel-Sigmoid gates at multiple granularities: macro-gates to prune entire attention heads and FFN blocks, and micro-gates to selectively prune intra-head dimensions and FFN neurons. By optimizing both levels simultaneously, HiAP addresses both the memory-bound overhead of loading large matrices and the compute-bound mathematical operations. HiAP naturally converges to stable sub-networks using a loss function that incorporates both structural feasibility penalties and analytical FLOPs. Extensive experiments on ImageNet demonstrate that HiAP organically discovers highly efficient architectures, and achieves a competitive accuracy-efficiency Pareto frontier for models like DeiT-Small, matching the performance of sophisticated multi-stage methods while significantly simplifying the deployment pipeline. Published: 2026-03-12T17:45:38+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.762 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Andy Li; Aiden Durrant; Milan Markovic; Georgios Leontidis

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.762 (consider)

Vision Transformers require significant computational resources and memory bandwidth, severely limiting their deployment on edge devices. While recent structured pruning methods successfully reduce theoretical FLOPs, they typically operate at a single structural granularity and rely on complex, multi-stage pipelines with post-hoc thresholding to satisfy sparsity budgets. In this paper, we propose Hierarchical Auto-Pruning (HiAP), a continuous relaxation framework that discovers optimal sub-networks in a single end-to-end training phase without requiring manual importance heuristics or predefined per-layer sparsity targets. HiAP introduces stochastic Gumbel-Sigmoid gates at multiple granularities: macro-gates to prune entire attention heads and FFN blocks, and micro-gates to selectively prune intra-head dimensions and FFN neurons. By optimizing both levels simultaneously, HiAP addresses both the memory-bound overhead of loading large matrices and the compute-bound mathematical operations. HiAP naturally converges to stable sub-networks using a loss function that incorporates both structural feasibility penalties and analytical FLOPs. Extensive experiments on ImageNet demonstrate that HiAP organically discovers highly efficient architectures, and achieves a competitive accuracy-efficiency Pareto frontier for models like DeiT-Small, matching the performance of sophisticated multi-stage methods while significantly simplifying the deployment pipeline.
